qa_pairs.question: How do you connect to a PostgreSQL database server on Fir from your desktop after setting up an SSH tunnel?
qa_pairs.answer: After establishing an SSH tunnel, you can connect to a PostgreSQL database server from your desktop using the command: `psql -h 127.0.0.1 -p PORT -U <your username> -d <your database>`. You should replace `PORT` with the specific port number chosen for the tunnel, `<your username>` with your account username, and `<your database>` with the desired database name.

qa_pairs.question: How do you connect to a MySQL database server on Fir from your desktop after setting up an SSH tunnel?
qa_pairs.answer: Once an SSH tunnel is established, you can connect to a MySQL database server from your desktop by running the command: `mysql -h 127.0.0.1 -P PORT -u <your username> --protocol=TCP -p`. Remember to replace `PORT` with the chosen port number for the tunnel and `<your username>` with your account username. MySQL will then prompt you for a password.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended maximum value for the local port number when setting up an SSH tunnel to connect to a database server on Fir from your desktop?
qa_pairs.answer: When setting up an SSH tunnel to connect to a database server from your desktop, the port number you choose (PORT) for your local host should not be bigger than 32768 (2^15).
